title: perymenium gypsophilum
intro: Perymenium gypsophilum, a flowering plant species native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States, is a member of the Asteraceae family and is also known as the Gypsum Perymenium. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoid compounds. The health benefits associated with Perymenium gypsophilum include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as alleviation of menstrual cramps and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Perymenium gypsophilum can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are also used topically to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne.
